attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user

   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename

message.content:
I had a disscusion with web-claude, https://claude.ai/share/6243d643-29ea-4267-bc4a-fe112ba2d5aa,
and using init/mod.rs is passe and from a practical matter with multiple subcommands they'll be
module mod.rs file making fuzzy searches impractical. Instead it suggests we keep src/<mod-name>.rs
but add src/<mod-name>/tests.rs. Of course now there are multiple tests.rs maybe we can have
"#[cfg(<mod-name>)] mod <mod-name>_tests;" thoughts?

content.text:
Agree on the layout, disagree on the rename.

**Non-mod.rs is the right call.** Modern Rust (2018+) prefers `src/init.rs` + `src/init/tests.rs` over `src/init/mod.rs` — exactly because every `mod.rs` looks identical in fuzzy finders. `cargo new` even generates this style. So `src/init.rs` keeps the module's code, and `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` resolves to `src/init/tests.rs`. No conflict — `init::tests` and `push::tests` are different namespaces.

**Don't rename to `<mod-name>_tests.rs`.** Multiple `tests.rs` files isn't the same problem as multiple `mod.rs`. `mod.rs` is an *entry point* — you open it expecting the module's main code. `tests.rs` is leaf content; fuzzy finders show the path (`init/tests.rs`, `push/tests.rs`, `sync/tests.rs`) and the directory disambiguates naturally. Renaming breaks the directory association without buying anything, and it's non-idiomatic.

**Implication for 0.43.0:** 0.43.0-1 already landed `src/init/mod.rs`. If we want consistency, that needs to be reshaped to `src/init.rs` + `src/init/tests.rs` — either as a fixup squashed into -1, or as -2 before doing push.rs. Push/sync/common would then follow the non-mod.rs pattern from the start.

Want me to lay out the fixup options for 0.43.0-1?

content.content:
200	### Goals
201	
202	- Shrink production files so the actual code is easier
203	  to navigate (`init.rs` is 2576 lines today, ~1093 of
204	  which are tests).
205	- Establish a layout shape that future cycles can keep
206	  using (further splitting into `cli.rs` / `ops.rs` per
207	  the ops-layer architecture, etc.).
208	- Done as one cycle so the four files end up
209	  consistent rather than half-converted across multiple
210	  cycles.
211	
212	### Per-file shape
213	
214	```
215	src/X.rs
216	  ↓
217	src/X/
218	  mod.rs               ← production code from src/X.rs
219	  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
220	  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
221	                         integration_tests (push, sync)
222	```
223	
224	In `src/X/mod.rs` after extraction:
225	
226	```rust
227	// ...production code...
228	
229	#[cfg(test)]
230	mod tests;
231	
232	#[cfg(test)]
233	mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
234	```
235	
236	In `src/X/tests.rs`:
237	
238	```rust
239	//! Unit tests for the X module.
240	
241	use super::*;
242	// ...test fns...
243	```
244	
245	### Sub-step ladder
246	
247	Size-descending order (init has the most test bulk;
248	common is borderline):
249	
250	- 0.43.0-0 plan + version bump + this section +
251	  todo.md ladder
252	- 0.43.0-1 src/init.rs → src/init/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
253	  (~1093 test lines)
254	- 0.43.0-2 src/push.rs → src/push/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
255	  integration_tests.rs} (~784 test lines + integration
256	  block)
257	- 0.43.0-3 src/sync.rs → src/sync/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
258	  integration_tests.rs} (~600 test lines, multiple
259	  `#[cfg(test)]` blocks)
260	- 0.43.0-4 src/common.rs → src/common/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
261	  (~384 test lines, borderline — call after seeing
262	  the first three land)
263	- 0.43.0 close-out
264	
265	### Order rationale
266	
267	`init` is the worked example: largest test bulk and the
268	file most-discussed in CLAUDE.md. Getting the pattern
269	right there sets the template for the others. The bot
270	thinks `common` is worth keeping for consistency but
271	reasonable to drop after seeing the first three land —
272	its 384 lines are below the threshold where extraction
273	visibly helps.
274	
275	### Per-substep contract
276	
277	Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`:
278	
279	- Run `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D

### Goals

- Shrink production files so the actual code is easier
  to navigate (`init.rs` is 2576 lines today, ~1093 of
  which are tests).
- Establish a layout shape that future cycles can keep
  using (further splitting into `cli.rs` / `ops.rs` per
  the ops-layer architecture, etc.).
- Done as one cycle so the four files end up
  consistent rather than half-converted across multiple
  cycles.

  ↓
src/X/
  mod.rs               ← production code from src/X.rs
  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod

```

// ...production code...

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests;

#[cfg(test)]
mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
```

In `src/X/tests.rs`:

```rust
//! Unit tests for the X module.

use super::*;
// ...test fns...
```

Size-descending order (init has the most test bulk;
common is borderline):

`init` is the worked example: largest test bulk and the

visibly helps.

input.new_string:
### Per-file shape

Non-mod.rs layout (Rust 2018+ idiom): the production
file keeps its top-level path; a sibling directory holds
children. `src/X.rs` and `src/X/` coexist — Rust resolves
`mod foo;` declared in `src/X.rs` to `src/X/foo.rs`.

```
src/X.rs               ← production code (unchanged path)
src/X/
  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
                         integration_tests (push, sync)
```

In `src/X.rs` after extraction:

```rust
// ...production code...

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests;

#[cfg(test)]
mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
```

In `src/X/tests.rs`:

```rust
//! Unit tests for the X module.

use super::*;
// ...test fns...
```

**Why non-mod.rs.** The `mod.rs` style names every
module's entry point identically — fuzzy finders show a
column of `mod.rs` rows that the reader must
disambiguate by directory. Multiple `tests.rs` files
don't have the same pain (the file is leaf content; the
directory tells the reader which module's tests they
are). Modern Rust (`cargo new`, RFC 2126) defaults to
non-mod.rs for exactly this reason.

12	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
13	///
14	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
15	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
16	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
17	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
18	///
19	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
20	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
21	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
22	///
23	/// Repo set is resolved from `--scope`:
24	///
25	/// - Explicit `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — keyword forms
26	///   resolve via the project root's `.vc-config.toml`; the path
27	///   form syncs that one repo (single-repo mode).
28	/// - No `--scope` — workspace-default scope:
29	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
30	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`

If you want sync-check as standard before every sub-step commit (and squash), that should be codified in:

- `CLAUDE.md` Pre-commit checklist (currently items 1-9, no sync step).

Two reasonable framings:

2. **Use `vc-x1 push --dry-run` instead of running the cycle by hand** — it runs the formal preflight (sync + fmt + clippy + test) end-to-end, mirrors what real push will do, single command. The downside: dry-run doesn't include `cargo install --locked` retest, so we'd still want that separately.
